description: Rice is a staple cereal crop that thrives in flooded paddy fields with consistently high soil moisture. Ideal growing temperature is 25-35°C with high humidity.
recommendation: Maintain standing water in paddies during vegetative growth. Irrigate when soil moisture drops below 40%. Drain fields 2 weeks before harvest.

description: Tomato plants are sensitive to moisture fluctuations especially during flowering and fruit set. They prefer well-drained soil with moderate moisture.
recommendation: Irrigate when soil moisture falls below 28%. Use drip irrigation to avoid leaf wetting. Reduce watering during fruit ripening to improve flavor.

description: Chili pepper plants prefer moderate moisture with excellent drainage. Overwatering causes root rot and reduced capsaicin production.
recommendation: Use short irrigation cycles when rainfall has contributed moisture. Target soil moisture of 30-35%. Mulch to retain moisture in hot weather.

description: Leafy greens like lettuce and spinach need frequent but light watering. They are shallow-rooted and wilt quickly in dry conditions.
recommendation: Check the 24-hour average moisture before irrigating. Keep soil moisture above 42%. Provide shade cloth when temperatures exceed 30°C.

description: Maize (corn) can tolerate brief dry periods but sustained water stress during tasseling reduces yield dramatically.
recommendation: Activate irrigation when threshold gap becomes strongly negative. Critical irrigation period is during silking and grain fill stages.

description: Sugarcane is a high-water-demand crop requiring consistent moisture throughout its 12-18 month growth cycle. Drought stress reduces sucrose content.
recommendation: Irrigate every 7-10 days during dry periods. Soil moisture should not drop below 35%. Reduce irrigation 4-6 weeks before harvest to increase sugar content.

description: Tea plants require consistent moisture and thrive in cool highland climates. Water stress reduces leaf quality and yield of tender shoots.
recommendation: Maintain soil moisture between 35-50%. Use sprinkler irrigation during dry spells. Mulch with tea prunings to conserve moisture.

description: Coconut palms are drought-tolerant once established but young palms need regular irrigation. Nut production increases with adequate moisture.
recommendation: Irrigate young palms during dry season. For mature palms, provide basin irrigation when soil moisture drops below 25%. Drip irrigation is most efficient.

description: Banana plants have high water requirements due to large leaf surface area. They are very sensitive to drought during bunch development.
recommendation: Irrigate twice weekly during dry periods. Maintain soil moisture above 38%. Use mulch to retain moisture. Avoid waterlogging which causes root rot.

description: Onion has shallow roots and requires frequent light irrigation. Overwatering during bulb maturation causes splitting and storage rot.
recommendation: Irrigate when top 15cm of soil dries. Keep moisture at 25-30% during bulb formation. Stop irrigation 2 weeks before harvest for better curing.

description: Potato tubers need consistent moisture during tuber initiation and bulking. Water stress causes knobby or cracked tubers.
recommendation: Irrigate to maintain soil moisture between 30-40%. Avoid wet-dry cycles which cause growth cracks. Hill up soil to protect developing tubers.

description: Carrots need deep consistent moisture for straight root development. Irregular watering causes forking and cracking.
recommendation: Maintain soil moisture at 30-35% consistently. Use drip irrigation. Avoid surface crusting by light frequent watering during germination.

description: Cabbage needs steady moisture throughout growth. Water stress during head formation leads to small loose heads and bolting.
recommendation: Irrigate when soil moisture drops below 35%. Apply 25-30mm per week. Use furrow or drip irrigation to minimize head rot diseases.

description: Green beans and dry beans are moderately drought tolerant but need irrigation during flowering and pod fill for good yields.
recommendation: Irrigate when soil moisture falls below 25%. Critical period is flowering through pod fill. Avoid overhead irrigation to prevent foliar diseases.

description: Groundnut (peanut) needs well-drained soil with moderate moisture. Critical water periods are flowering and peg penetration.
recommendation: Irrigate to maintain 25-35% soil moisture. Ensure adequate moisture during pegging stage. Reduce irrigation near harvest for easier lifting.

description: Soybean is moderately drought tolerant but yields drop significantly when water stress occurs during pod filling stage.
recommendation: Irrigate when soil moisture drops below 30%. Critical period is R3-R5 (pod development). Soybeans fix nitrogen so coordinate with fertilizer plan.

description: Cucumber plants have high water needs and shallow roots. Irregular watering causes bitter fruit and poor shape.
recommendation: Maintain consistent soil moisture above 35%. Use drip irrigation with mulch. Irrigate daily during fruit production in hot weather.

description: Watermelon needs deep but infrequent irrigation. Overwatering during ripening dilutes sugar content and causes fruit splitting.
recommendation: Irrigate deeply when soil moisture drops below 25%. Reduce watering by 50% two weeks before harvest to concentrate sugars.

description: Okra is heat-loving and moderately drought tolerant. It performs well in tropical climates with adequate moisture.
recommendation: Irrigate when soil moisture falls below 30%. Okra tolerates heat well but needs consistent moisture during flowering for continuous pod production.

description: Eggplant (brinjal) needs warm conditions and consistent moisture. Water stress causes flower drop and small bitter fruits.
recommendation: Maintain soil moisture at 30-38%. Use mulch to keep soil temperature stable. Irrigate every 3-4 days during fruiting stage.

description: Pumpkin is a spreading vine crop that needs deep watering. Shallow frequent watering encourages surface roots.
recommendation: Water deeply once or twice per week. Maintain soil moisture at 28-35%. Reduce irrigation as fruits mature to improve storage quality.

description: Sweet potato is drought tolerant once established but needs moisture during root initiation. Excess water reduces tuber quality.
recommendation: Irrigate during first 6 weeks after planting. Maintain soil moisture at 25-30% during root bulking. Avoid waterlogging which causes rot.

description: Cassava is extremely drought tolerant and can survive extended dry periods. However adequate moisture increases root starch content.
recommendation: Irrigate during establishment phase only in severe drought. Cassava rarely needs supplemental irrigation once established.

description: Mango trees need water stress during flowering to promote fruit set. Irrigation during fruit development increases size and quality.
recommendation: Withhold irrigation 2 months before expected flowering. Resume irrigation after fruit set. Apply 100-150 liters per tree per week during fruit development.

description: Papaya trees are fast-growing and need consistent moisture. They cannot tolerate waterlogging which causes root rot and stem collapse.
recommendation: Irrigate every 3-4 days in dry weather. Maintain soil moisture at 35-40%. Ensure excellent drainage. Apply mulch around the base.

description: Guava is a hardy fruit tree that tolerates various soil moisture conditions. Regulated deficit irrigation improves fruit sweetness.
recommendation: Irrigate when soil moisture drops below 25%. Withhold water partially before harvest to improve fruit quality and shelf life.

description: Passion fruit vines need consistent moisture but are susceptible to root diseases in waterlogged soils.
recommendation: Maintain soil moisture at 30-38%. Use trellised drip irrigation. Ensure good drainage. Mulch vine base to regulate soil temperature.

description: Turmeric needs high moisture during active growth phase. The rhizomes develop best with consistent warm wet conditions.
recommendation: Irrigate every 5-7 days during vegetative growth. Maintain soil moisture above 40%. Reduce irrigation one month before harvest.

description: Ginger requires consistent moisture for rhizome development. Drought causes fibrous tough rhizomes with reduced quality.
recommendation: Irrigate every 4-5 days. Maintain soil moisture at 35-45%. Use mulching extensively. Avoid waterlogging which causes rhizome rot.

description: Black pepper vines are moisture-loving climbers that need humidity for berry development. Dry conditions cause berry drop.
recommendation: Irrigate vines during dry season. Maintain soil moisture above 35%. Use basin irrigation around the support pole. Mulch generously.

description: Cinnamon trees are drought tolerant once established but young plants need regular moisture. Quality improves with moderate stress.
recommendation: Water young plants weekly. Established trees need irrigation only during severe drought. Maintain soil moisture at 25-35%.

description: Clove trees thrive in humid tropical conditions. Flowering is triggered by a dry period followed by moisture.
recommendation: Allow mild dry stress before expected flowering. Resume regular irrigation after bud formation. Maintain 30-40% soil moisture during fruit development.

description: Mushrooms require high humidity environments with specific substrate moisture levels. Air moisture is more critical than soil moisture.
recommendation: Maintain substrate moisture at 60-65%. Keep growing area humidity above 85%. Mist frequently rather than heavy watering. Ensure good air exchange.

description: During drought conditions paddy fields face severe yield losses. Alternate wetting and drying (AWD) saves 20-30% water with minimal yield impact.
recommendation: Implement AWD technique by allowing field water to drop 15cm below soil surface before re-flooding. Install field water tubes to monitor water depth.

description: Drip irrigation delivers water directly to the root zone reducing evaporation losses by 30-50% compared to flood irrigation.
recommendation: Install drip lines within 30cm of plant base. Run system for 30-60 minutes per session. Schedule early morning irrigation to minimize evaporation losses.

description: Organic mulching reduces soil evaporation by 25-50% and moderates soil temperature fluctuations. Effective for all tropical crops.
recommendation: Apply 5-10cm layer of organic mulch (rice straw, coconut husks, leaves). Replenish monthly. Keep mulch 10cm away from plant stems to prevent stem rot.

description: Continuous soil moisture monitoring enables precision irrigation scheduling. Sensors should be placed at root zone depth for each crop.
recommendation: Place sensors at 15cm depth for shallow-rooted crops and 30cm for deep-rooted crops. Calibrate monthly. Use trend data to predict irrigation needs 6-12 hours ahead.

description: Nitrogen application timing directly affects irrigation needs. Over-fertilization with nitrogen increases vegetative growth and water demand.
recommendation: Split nitrogen application into 3-4 doses during crop growth. Apply after irrigation when soil is moist. Avoid applying before heavy expected rainfall.

description: Phosphorus promotes root development and helps plants access water from deeper soil layers. Deficiency shows as purple leaf discoloration.
recommendation: Apply phosphorus during land preparation. Maintain soil pH between 6.0-7.0 for optimal phosphorus availability. Use rock phosphate for slow-release application.

description: Potassium strengthens cell walls and regulates stomata helping plants retain water during heat stress. Critical for fruit quality.
recommendation: Apply potassium sulfate during fruit development stage. Split applications into 2-3 doses. Potassium improves shelf life and sugar content in fruits.

description: Well-decomposed compost improves soil water-holding capacity by 20% and reduces irrigation frequency. Essential for sandy soils.
recommendation: Apply 5-10 tons per hectare of decomposed compost during land preparation. Incorporate into top 20cm of soil. Supplement with inorganic fertilizers as needed.

description: Leaf blight is a fungal disease aggravated by overhead irrigation and high humidity. Common in rice tomato and potato crops.
recommendation: Avoid overhead irrigation during disease pressure. Ensure good air circulation. Apply copper-based fungicide preventively. Remove infected plant material.

description: Root rot is caused by waterlogging and poor drainage. Affects most tropical crops especially during heavy monsoon periods.
recommendation: Improve drainage channels. Avoid over-irrigation. Raise planting beds in heavy soils. Apply Trichoderma biological control agent to soil before planting.

description: Aphids thrive in moderate temperature and humidity. They transmit viral diseases and reduce plant vigor through sap feeding.
recommendation: Monitor undersides of leaves weekly. Use neem oil spray at first sign. Introduce ladybug beetles as biological control. Avoid excessive nitrogen fertilization.

description: Fruit flies damage ripening fruits causing premature drop and rot. They are attracted by fermented fruit odors.
recommendation: Set up methyl eugenol traps at 10 per hectare. Harvest fruits at mature green stage. Collect and destroy fallen fruits. Maintain field hygiene.

description: Whiteflies are vectors for plant viruses and thrive in warm dry conditions. Common in tomato chili and cucumber crops.
recommendation: Install yellow sticky traps for monitoring. Use reflective mulch to deter whiteflies. Apply insecticidal soap or neem extract at early infestation stage.

description: Soil pH affects nutrient availability and microbial activity. Most crops prefer pH 6.0-7.0. Acidic soils need liming.
recommendation: Test soil pH before each planting season. Apply agricultural lime at 2-4 tons per hectare for acidic soils. Mix lime into soil 2-4 weeks before planting.

description: Crop rotation breaks pest and disease cycles reduces soil nutrient depletion and improves soil structure.
recommendation: Rotate between legumes cereals and vegetables. Follow heavy feeders (cabbage maize) with nitrogen-fixing legumes (bean groundnut). Maintain 3-year rotation minimum.

description: Collecting and storing rainwater reduces dependence on irrigation infrastructure. One hectare can yield 5000-10000 cubic meters annually.
recommendation: Install rainwater collection systems from farm building roofs. Build farm ponds with 500-1000 cubic meter capacity. Use stored water during dry spells.

description: Temperatures above 35°C cause pollen sterility in most crops. Combined with low humidity this creates critical yield loss risk.
recommendation: Schedule irrigation during evening to reduce soil temperature. Use shade cloth for sensitive crops. Apply kaolin clay spray to reflect sunlight from leaves.

description: Excess soil salinity from over-irrigation or poor drainage reduces crop water uptake. White salt crusts on soil surface indicate problems.
recommendation: Leach salts by applying 20% more water than crop needs quarterly. Improve drainage. Apply gypsum at 2-5 tons per hectare. Plant salt-tolerant crops in affected areas.

description: Cover crops like mucuna and sun hemp protect soil from erosion improve water infiltration and add organic matter when incorporated.
recommendation: Plant cover crops during fallow periods. Incorporate into soil 2-3 weeks before main crop planting. Leave residue as mulch for moisture conservation.
